original_abstract:
This volume examines the complex trajectory of higher education reforms in Romania between 2009 and 2013, a period marked by the tension between international commitments within the Bologna Process and persistent national structural challenges. Drawing on extensive research conducted under the "Higher Education Evidence-Based Policy Making" project, the book provides a multidimensional analysis of the implementation of Law 1/2011 and its subsequent impact on the university landscape.

The chapters explore critical policy domains, including quality assurance internalization, internationalization strategies, equity and access for under-represented groups, and the transition toward student-centered learning. By integrating perspectives from both national and international experts, the contributors assess why certain reforms—such as internal quality culture and social inclusion mechanisms—have faced institutional resistance or ritualistic compliance. Utilizing various case studies and data-driven impact assessments, the volume highlights the necessity of building administrative capacity and fostering a stable, evidence-based policy environment. Ultimately, the book serves as both a diagnostic tool for the Romanian system's recent history and a strategic guide for future reforms aimed at enhancing global competitiveness and institutional accountability.
humanized_abstract:
This book analyses the dynamics of higher education reform in Romania between 2009 and 2013, a period in which the tension between the Europeanisation of higher education through the Bologna Process and the national structural obstacles rose to a peak. Focusing on the research carried out in the framework of the Higher Education Evidence-Based Policy Making project, the book gives a plural perspective on the implementation of Law 1/2011 and its effects on higher education in Romania.

The chapters are dedicated to essential policy areas: quality assurance internationalization, internationalization strategies, issues related to access and equity for underrepresented groups, as well as student-centred learning. By bringing together both national and international expertise, this volume redresses the balance of perspectives on the reasons behind the difficulties that certain reforms have encountered at the institutional level – such as the embedding of an internal quality culture and social exclusion mechanisms – and the ways in which internationalisation and access have been implemented at the country level, as evidenced by the case studies and impact assessment data included in the volume. This book provides a combination of a retrospective diagnosis of the recent evolution of the Romanian higher education system and a forward-looking roadmap that can serve as a reference point for the continuation of the modernisation process of the sector in Romania in order to support the country’s continued integration into the global knowledge economy and to improve the accountability of Higher Education Institutions.
